SEARCH
Package com.dua3.utility.io.imp

Class FileObjectStore

java.lang.Object
com.dua3.utility.io.imp.FileObjectStore
All Implemented Interfaces:
ObjectStore, ReadableObjectStore, WritableObjectStore, AutoCloseable
public final class FileObjectStore extends Object implements ObjectStore
A file-based implementation of the ObjectStore interface, which allows for storing and managing objects (files and folders) within a predetermined root directory. This implementation ensures path validation and enforces that all operations remain confined to the root directory of the store.

This class is immutable and thread-safe, meaning its methods can be called safely by multiple threads concurrently.

  • Nested Class Summary

  • Constructor Details

    • FileObjectStore

      public FileObjectStore(Path root) throws IOException
      Constructs a FileObjectStore with the specified root directory. It normalizes and ensures the creation of the root directory as a valid absolute path.
      Parameters:
      root - the path to the root directory of the file object store; must not be null
      Throws:
      IOException - if an I/O error occurs while creating or accessing the directory

  • Method Details

    • getRoot

      public URI getRoot()
      Description copied from interface: ReadableObjectStore
      Retrieve the root URI that serves as the base location for objects managed by this storage. The URI returned will always be an absolute URI.
      Specified by:
      getRoot in interface ReadableObjectStore
      Specified by:
      getRoot in interface WritableObjectStore
      Returns:
      the root URI representing the base location of the storage

    • list

      public Stream<ObjectStore.ObjectInfo> list(URI path) throws IOException
      Description copied from interface: ReadableObjectStore
      Lists the objects located at the specified path within the storage. If the path corresponds to a folder, the method will provide a stream of ObjectStore.ObjectInfo objects representing the metadata of the folder's contents.

      Notes:

      • The caller must close the returned stream.
      • Implementations must ensure that the object graph is acyclic, or define how cycles are handled.
      Specified by:
      list in interface ReadableObjectStore
      Parameters:
      path - the URI of the folder or object to be listed; must not be absolute.
      Returns:
      a stream of ObjectStore.ObjectInfo instances representing the objects under the specified path
      Throws:
      IOException - if an I/O error occurs while accessing the storage

    • write

      public long write(URI path, InputStream in, ObjectStore.OutputOption... options) throws IOException
      Description copied from interface: WritableObjectStore
      Writes data from the specified InputStream to an object located at the specified URI path within the object store. If the object already exists, its contents may be overwritten.
      Specified by:
      write in interface WritableObjectStore
      Parameters:
      path - the URI specifying the location where the input data should be written
      in - the InputStream providing the data to be written
      options - the ObjectStore.ObjectInfo specifying how to handle existing objects at the output location; when none are present, ObjectStore.OutputOption.CREATE_NEW is used.
      Returns:
      the number of bytes successfully written to the object
      Throws:
      IOException - if an I/O error occurs during the write operation

    • write

      public long write(URI path, byte[] data, int from, int to, ObjectStore.OutputOption... options) throws IOException
      Description copied from interface: WritableObjectStore
      Writes data from the specified InputStream to an object located at the specified URI path within the object store. If the object already exists, its contents may be overwritten.
      Specified by:
      write in interface WritableObjectStore
      Parameters:
      path - the URI specifying the location where the input data should be written
      data - the byte array containing the data to be written
      from - the starting index in the data array to be written (inclusive)
      to - the ending index in the data array to be written (exclusive)
      options - the ObjectStore.OutputOption specifying how to handle existing objects at the output location; when none are present, ObjectStore.OutputOption.CREATE_NEW is used.
      Returns:
      the number of bytes successfully written to the object
      Throws:
      IOException - if an I/O error occurs during the write operation

    • openInputStream

      public InputStream openInputStream(URI path) throws IOException
      Description copied from interface: ReadableObjectStore
      Opens an input stream to read data from the specified URI. The method returns an InputStream for reading the contents of the object at the given path.
      Specified by:
      openInputStream in interface ReadableObjectStore
      Parameters:
      path - the URI of the object to be read
      Returns:
      an InputStream to read data from the specified URI
      Throws:
      IOException - if the object at the path is not a data object or an I/O error occurs while opening the input stream

    • openOutputStream

      public OutputStream openOutputStream(URI path, ObjectStore.OutputOption... options) throws IOException
      Description copied from interface: WritableObjectStore
      Opens an output stream to write data to the specified URI. This method provides an OutputStream for writing data to the object located at the specified path. If the object already exists, its contents may be overwritten.

      If any I/O error occurs while opening the output stream, the operation aborts and the store may be partially modified.

      Specified by:
      openOutputStream in interface WritableObjectStore
      Parameters:
      path - the URI specifying the location where the output data will be written
      options - the ObjectStore.OutputOption specifying how to handle existing objects at the output location; when none are present, ObjectStore.OutputOption.CREATE_NEW is used.
      Returns:
      an OutputStream for writing data to the specified URI
      Throws:
      IOException - if an I/O error occurs while opening the output stream

    • createFolder

      public void createFolder(URI path) throws IOException
      Description copied from interface: WritableObjectStore
      Creates a folder at the specified URI path within the object store. If the folder already exists, this method does nothing. Missing parent folders are created as needed. If a non-folder object already exists at the specified path, an exception is thrown.
      Specified by:
      createFolder in interface WritableObjectStore
      Parameters:
      path - the URI representing the path where the folder will be created
      Throws:
      IOException - if an I/O error occurs while creating the folder

    • removeFolder

      public void removeFolder(URI path) throws IOException
      Description copied from interface: ObjectStore
      Removes the specified folder from the object store. Fails if the folder is not empty.
      Specified by:
      removeFolder in interface ObjectStore
      Parameters:
      path - the URI representing the path of the folder to be removed; must not be an absolute URI and must point within the root of the storage.
      Throws:
      IOException - if an I/O error occurs during the removal process.

    • getInfo

      public Optional<ObjectStore.ObjectInfo> getInfo(URI path) throws IOException
      Description copied from interface: ReadableObjectStore
      Get info about the object pointed to by the provided URI.
      Specified by:
      getInfo in interface ReadableObjectStore
      Parameters:
      path - the URI to check
      Returns:
      An Optional containing an ObjectStore.ObjectInfo for the object at the URI, or an empty Optional if the URI does not point to an existing object.
      Throws:
      IOException - if an I/O error occurs while attempting to retrieve the object info.

    • delete

      public void delete(URI path) throws IOException
      Description copied from interface: ObjectStore
      Deletes the object or folder located at the specified URI path within the object store. If the specified path corresponds to a non-empty folder, an IOException is thrown.
      Specified by:
      delete in interface ObjectStore
      Parameters:
      path - the URI representing the location of the object or folder to be deleted
      Throws:
      IOException - if an I/O error occurs while performing the delete operation

    • deleteRecursively

      public void deleteRecursively(URI path) throws IOException
      Description copied from interface: ObjectStore
      Deletes an object at the specified path recursively. If the path refers to a folder, all its contents, including subfolders, are deleted. If the path refers to a file, the file is deleted. If any deletion fails, the operation aborts and the store may be partially modified.
      Specified by:
      deleteRecursively in interface ObjectStore
      Parameters:
      path - the URI representing the location of the object or folder to be deleted
      Throws:
      IOException - if an I/O error occurs while deleting the contents

    • close

      public void close()
      Specified by:
      close in interface AutoCloseable
